items.statement: The apt command provides a high-level commandline interface for the package management system and is intended as an end user interface.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt is described as a high-level end user interface for package management.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt upgrade command is used to install available upgrades of packages currently installed on the system.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt upgrade installs available upgrades for installed packages.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt upgrade command never removes existing installed packages.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt upgrade may add dependencies but will not remove installed packages.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt full-upgrade command may remove currently installed packages if that is needed to upgrade the system as a whole.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt full-upgrade can remove installed packages when required for the upgrade.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: Removing a package with apt leaves usually small modified user configuration files behind.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt remove leaves modified user configuration files behind.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt autoremove command removes packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies and are no longer needed.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt autoremove removes no-longer-needed automatically installed dependency packages.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt search command can be used to search for given regex terms in the list of available packages and display matches.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt search looks for regex terms in available packages and shows matches.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt list command can display installed, upgradeable, or all available versions of packages.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt list supports installed, upgradeable, and all-versions views.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt edit-sources command lets you edit sources.list files in your preferred text editor while providing basic sanity checks.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt edit-sources edits sources.list files with basic sanity checks.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get update command resynchronizes the package index files from their sources.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get update refreshes package index files from configured sources.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: An apt-get update should always be performed before apt-get upgrade or apt-get dist-upgrade.
items.source_quote_or_summary: The apt-get man page says update should always come before upgrade or dist-upgrade.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get install command retrieves and installs packages required by the specified package or packages.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get install also brings in required packages for the requested package set.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get remove command removes packages but leaves configuration files on the system.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get remove leaves configuration files behind.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get purge command removes packages and deletes configuration files too.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get purge removes packages and their configuration files.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get download command downloads the given binary package into the current directory.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get download fetches a binary package into the current directory.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get clean command removes everything but the lock file from /var/cache/apt/archives/ and /var/cache/apt/archives/partial/.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get clean clears retrieved package files but keeps the lock file.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get autoclean command removes only retrieved package files that can no longer be downloaded.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get autoclean targets obsolete cached package files instead of clearing everything.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get check command is a diagnostic tool that updates the package cache and checks for broken dependencies.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get check updates cache metadata and checks dependency consistency.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get --download-only option retrieves package files but does not unpack or install them.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get --download-only fetches packages without unpacking or installing them.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get --only-upgrade option does not install new packages and only upgrades packages that are already installed.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get --only-upgrade limits install to packages that already exist on the system.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: The apt-get --no-upgrade option prevents command-line packages from being upgraded if they are already installed.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get --no-upgrade blocks upgrades of already installed command-line packages.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: When used with upgrade, the apt-get --with-new-pkgs option allows installing new dependencies but never removes packages.
items.source_quote_or_summary: apt-get --with-new-pkgs can add new dependency packages during upgrade without allowing removals.
items.notes: APT behavior is distribution-specific to Debian-family systems and may vary by release.

items.statement: DNF is a package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions.
items.source_quote_or_summary: DNF is documented as a package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: DNF returns 0 when the operation was successful.
items.source_quote_or_summary: DNF uses exit code 0 for success.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: DNF uses exit code 100 for the check-update case.
items.source_quote_or_summary: DNF reserves exit code 100 for check-update.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: The dnf --allowerasing option allows erasing installed packages to resolve dependencies.
items.source_quote_or_summary: dnf --allowerasing permits package removals during dependency resolution.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: The dnf --assumeno option automatically answers no for all questions.
items.source_quote_or_summary: dnf --assumeno declines prompts automatically.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: The dnf --best option forces DNF to consider only the latest packages for directly requested packages and fail if the latest version cannot be installed.
items.source_quote_or_summary: dnf --best prefers latest versions for requested packages and fails when the latest cannot be installed.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: The dnf --cacheonly option runs entirely from the system cache and uses it even when it is expired.
items.source_quote_or_summary: dnf --cacheonly avoids cache refreshes and works from existing cache data.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.

items.statement: The dnf --disablerepo option temporarily disables active repositories for the current dnf command.
items.source_quote_or_summary: dnf --disablerepo only affects the current command invocation.
items.notes: DNF behavior is distribution-specific to RPM-based systems and plugin behavior can vary by environment.
